methodology: Single-phase BUILD-equivalent task run by each engine independently on identical content (a 1-line grammar fix in agents-config/evaluator.md:15). NOT a full pipeline comparison — that requires the Agent tool which is unavailable in the benchmarking environment. This measures raw engine cost for a task of the type BUILD phase routes to.
task_description: Add two 'the' articles to a bullet in agents-config/evaluator.md line 15 so it reads 'surface the error to the user'.

runs.outcome: PASS — correct edit, one-line change, no unintended mutations
runs.note: Codex CLI itself reports tokens used in stdout. wall_ms measured by shell millisecond timestamps around the exec call.

runs.outcome: PASS — correct edit on the n=1 fast-route pilot
runs.note: Per-phase tokens were null in that pilot (Agent tool unavailable). Agent aggregate total was 115,317 tokens for the full fast-route run (parse + build + gate + eval + orchestration).

direct_deltas.codex_wall_vs_claude_full_fast_run: 15,770 ms vs 77,289 ms — codex engine alone 4.90x faster than full claude pipeline, but this is not apples-to-apples since the claude full run includes parse+eval+orchestration overhead
direct_deltas.token_delta: not comparable — claude per-phase tokens are null. Codex 15,360 is end-to-end for the edit.

caveats: n=1. Different invocation paths (codex via CLI, claude via inline session). Environment overhead differs. Directional signal only. The fair 'full auto-resolve --engine auto vs --engine claude' measurement still requires a pipeline-level experiment with Agent access, which this environment cannot provide. Do NOT treat the 2x speedup as the general auto-vs-claude pipeline delta — BUILD and FIX phases are the ones that route to codex under --engine auto; EVAL, CHALLENGE, and others remain claude in both engine modes. Net pipeline savings are weighted by what fraction of wall-time BUILD+FIX consume, which varies by route and fix-round count.
implications: For single-phase 'do this simple edit' tasks, codex CLI is measurably faster than claude inline edit. Rough factor 2x.
This supports the benchmark-table routing decision (BUILD/FIX to codex on --engine auto) at least for trivial tasks. Strict-route complex tasks are untested.
Token cost comparison is still open — codex reports 15k for the edit; claude inline 'tokens per edit' is not directly measurable through current instrumentation.
